subtasks.description: Evaluate the contractor's access request against data governance and access control policies. Determine if temporary contractor access is permitted for full database backups and what data classification restrictions apply.

Reconcile the contractor's current access profile (role, tenure, clearance level) with the database backup classification (customer data sensitivity, PII scope) and the requested action (local download). Generate a risk posture assessment and recommend an approval decision (approve, deny, or conditional approval with safeguards).

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) policy_action (mid tier) evaluates governance rules for contractor data access, temporary access constraints, and backup download permissions. (2) cross_recon (mid tier) reconciles the contractor's access profile against data sensitivity and generates a risk-based recommendation. Both mid tier because they require multi-signal reasoning (policy evaluation + access context + data classification) but no extreme complexity. We do not add a separate policy_action for the final decision because the recommendation is generated as part of cross_recon. Plan is minimal: no sql_gen, mongo_query, or extract needed—this is purely an access control and governance decision on existing data, not a data retrieval or transformation query.

output.reason: All three applicable policies prohibit this action. POL-701 restricts database backup access exclusively to full-time employees with DBA role; contractor-dave holds neither status. POL-702 explicitly limits contractors to de-identified query results and prohibits raw data exports such as production_backup.tar.gz. POL-703 requires explicit project manager approval with 7-day advance notice for any contractor data access, which has not been established. The requested download of a full production customer database backup by a contractor represents a high-risk data governance violation across all evaluated policies.
applied_constraints: Access to production_backup.tar.gz denied for all contractor roles per POL-701
Raw data exports prohibited for contractors; only de-identified query results permitted per POL-702
No project manager approval on record and 7-day notice requirement not satisfied per POL-703
Incident should be logged and security team notified of unauthorized access attempt
Contractor may be redirected to submit a formal data access request through approved channels with PM sponsorship
If legitimate analysis need exists, contractor must work with a DBA to receive scoped, de-identified query results only
